title: Pour plating protocol for Emiliania huxleyi (single colonies)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"di0cd","text":"A protocol for generating single colonies of Emiliania huxleyi embedded in soft-agarose. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Autoclave 75 mL DDW with 0.75 g agarose
For 300 mL (= 7 plates): heat 225 mL FSW in a 50 °C water bath.
Cool autoclaved agar to 50 °C, mix with heated FSW, add F/2 stock and antibiotics if necessary. Swirl/mix well.
For each sample, pour 40 mL of FSW-agar mixture to a 50 mL conical tube.
Prepare the cells at desired cell concentration. For each sample prepare 0.5ml cells.
When you are ready, remove FSW-agar mix from 32 °C bath and allow to cool 1-2 degreesCritical: the mixture will start to solidify at 27-28 °C.
Add cell suspension to each tube, mix gently, then pour into plates.
Let solidify to a loose consistency
Transfer to growth room. Single colonies should appear within 10 days (without selection).
creator: Daniella Schatz
